title: Japanese sign target remains primary in every interface language
purpose: Keep the physical Japanese sign target and route shield visible while localized text explains the same movement.

limitations: The sign text is illustrative and is not release evidence for a real movement.

guidance.japanese_sign_text: B 湾岸線・横浜方面

ja-JP.display_text: 左側を進み、B 湾岸線・横浜方面へ
ja-JP.spoken_text: B 湾岸線、横浜方面へ
ja-JP.preserved_japanese_sign_text: B 湾岸線・横浜方面

zh-Hans.display_text: 保持左侧，跟随 B 湾岸线・横滨方向
zh-Hans.spoken_text: 保持左侧，跟随 B 湾岸线，横滨方向
zh-Hans.preserved_japanese_sign_text: B 湾岸線・横浜方面

en.display_text: Keep left for Route B toward Yokohama
en.spoken_text: Keep left for Route B toward Yokohama
en.preserved_japanese_sign_text: B 湾岸線・横浜方面

then.subject: presentation.kernel.phone.japanese_sign_text
then.expected: B 湾岸線・横浜方面
then.rationale: The phone must show the exact physical sign target, not only a translation.

then.subject: presentation.kernel.carplay.japanese_sign_text
then.expected: B 湾岸線・横浜方面
then.rationale: The same sign target is projected to CarPlay from shared state.

then.subject: presentation.kernel.phone.route_shields
then.rationale: Route shields are primary recognition aids for foreign and local drivers.

then.subject: presentation.kernel.phone.localized_display_text
then.expected: Keep left for Route B toward Yokohama
then.rationale: Localized prose explains rather than replaces the sign target.
